name: Barbell Overhead Press
resistance_profile: Standing vertical press; heavy front-delt and triceps loader.
cues: Press the bar and move the head 'through'
execution_steps: Set the bar in a rack at upper-chest height and grip just outside shoulder width.
Unrack and hold the bar on your front delts with elbows slightly in front and wrists stacked over the forearms.
Brace your core hard and squeeze your glutes to lock the torso.
Press the bar straight up, moving your head back slightly so the bar clears your face.
Once the bar passes your forehead, push your head 'through' and lock out with the bar over mid-foot.
Lower under control back to the front-delt shelf.
good_when: You want a heavy, easily progressable primary overhead press.
You are building overall shoulder and triceps strength and pressing power.
You brace well and want a standing, full-body pressing stimulus.
bad_when: You have lower-back issues aggravated by standing overhead loading.
You want a deep front-delt stretch (dumbbells provide more range).
Limited overhead mobility forces a painful lumbar arch to reach lockout.
